title: Compression-Native Backbone Audit
research_question: Can structured low-rank factorization inside the backbone preserve enough quality to become a real compression-native direction, rather than training a dense model and compressing later?

candidates.name: u1-factorized-attention-128
candidates.goal: Test whether attention projections are overbuilt for the compressed artifact.
candidates.hypothesis: Moderate factorization inside attention may save bytes and memory with less quality loss than expected.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether attention is a good compression-native target on the current backbone.
candidates.why_worthy: The leaderboard repeatedly suggests compression-aware structure matters; this is the cleanest first backbone test in that direction.

candidates.name: u2-factorized-attention-64
candidates.goal: Push attention factorization harder after the milder bracket.
candidates.hypothesis: Attention may tolerate a surprisingly aggressive factorization before quality collapses.
candidates.what_it_teaches: How steep the attention-factorization tradeoff is.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the correct bracket pair for deciding whether the idea is mildly useful or fundamentally strong.

candidates.name: u3-factorized-mlp-256
candidates.goal: Test whether MLP projections are the cleaner compression target.
candidates.hypothesis: The MLP may be wasting more dense projection structure than the attention block is.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the feedforward path is a better compression-native target than attention.
candidates.why_worthy: This attacks a different major cost center and helps answer where the backbone is actually overbuilt.

candidates.name: u4-factorized-mlp-128
candidates.hypothesis: The MLP may tolerate stronger factorization than attention because it is already a wide token-local transform.
candidates.what_it_teaches: How steep the MLP-factorization tradeoff is.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the right aggression bracket once the milder MLP factorization is represented.

candidates.name: u5-factorized-both-moderate
candidates.goal: Test whether modest factorization across both major dense subsystems works better than pushing one subsystem too hard.
candidates.hypothesis: A distributed compression-native cut may preserve quality better than a single aggressive cut.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether compression-native structure wants to be shared across attention and MLP together.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most justified combo inside the factorization family and directly answers whether the gains are subsystem-specific or shared.
candidates.description: factorize attention at rank 128 and MLP at rank 256 together
